hero.name: Daniil
hero.role: Early-stage founders: from idea to shipped product without the chaos
hero.description: I own the full path — discovery, architecture, dev coordination, and delivery. You get clarity and a working product, not just code.

fit.title: Is this for you?
forYouItems: You're pre-seed to Series A with a product idea but no technical co-founder
You've been burned by agencies that shipped code you can't maintain
You need someone who owns the outcome, not just writes tickets
notForYouItems: You need a junior developer for hourly tasks
You have a full tech team and just need extra hands
You're looking for the cheapest option

about.title: About Me
about.subtitle: Building Products, Not Just Code
about.description: I own the path from unclear requirements to shipped product. That means discovery, architecture decisions, coordinating developers, and communicating progress. I work well with incomplete information, tight timelines, and the realities of early-stage constraints.
about.creative: Beyond building systems, I explore pattern and movement through creative disciplines — dance and visual arts. The same thinking that helps me navigate legacy chaos or design elegant architectures comes from learning to find solutions where there's no obvious path.

beliefs.title: What I believe
items: Most MVP failures aren't technical — they're communication failures
The best architecture is the one your team can actually maintain
Clarity costs less than rework
Creativity emerges from constraints — true in code and in dance

contact.title: Let's Talk
contact.subtitle: Start a Conversation
contact.description: Book a quick call. I'll tell you if I can help — no pitch, just clarity.

footer.description: Tech partner helping founders turn ideas into shipped products.

leadMagnet.title: Technical Discovery Checklist for Early-Stage Founders
leadMagnet.subtitle: Free Resource
leadMagnet.description: A comprehensive checklist to validate your product idea, define MVP scope, and make critical technical decisions before you start building.

emailForm.title: Get Your Free Checklist
emailForm.subtitle: Enter your email to access the interactive checklist
emailForm.privacy: I respect your privacy. No spam, ever.

benefits.title: What you'll get:
items: 40+ actionable checklist items across 5 critical areas
Interactive progress tracking
Downloadable PDF for your team

problemDefinition.title: Problem Definition
problemDefinition.description: Validate the core problem before building anything
items: Documented 10+ conversations with target users about their pain points
Identified the #1 problem worth solving (not a nice-to-have)
Validated that users are willing to pay for a solution
Confirmed existing workarounds are painful enough to switch
Defined success metrics (revenue, retention, engagement)
Identified competing solutions and why they fail
Set a clear timeline for validation (3-6 months max)

mvpScope.title: MVP Scope
mvpScope.description: Define what to build first (and what to skip)
items: Listed all possible features, then cut 80% of them
Defined the single core workflow that delivers value
Identified which features are must-have vs nice-to-have
Set a realistic timeline (6-12 weeks for first version)
Determined success criteria for the MVP
Planned user onboarding flow
Defined what 'done' looks like
Prioritized technical debt vs speed tradeoffs

techStack.title: Tech Stack Decisions
techStack.description: Choose tools that enable speed without creating chaos
items: Selected proven technologies (not bleeding edge)
Ensured the stack matches team expertise
Evaluated hosting costs at scale
Planned for future scaling needs (but not over-engineering)
Decided on monolith vs microservices approach
Chose database that fits data model
Selected payment processor and integration complexity
Evaluated third-party services vs building in-house

teamNeeds.title: Team & Resources
teamNeeds.description: Determine who you need and when
items: Identified skills gap in current team
Decided between hiring, outsourcing, or technical partner
Set realistic budget for development
Planned for ongoing maintenance after launch
Determined code ownership and knowledge transfer needs
Evaluated agency vs freelancer vs full-time options
Set communication expectations and cadence

timeline.title: Timeline & Milestones
items: Broke down project into 2-week sprints
Defined deliverables for each milestone
Set demo dates to show progress to stakeholders
Planned buffer time for unexpected issues (25%+)
Scheduled user testing sessions
Determined go-live date and launch strategy
Planned post-launch support and iteration cycles
Set review points to assess progress vs goals

successMessage.title: Great progress!
successMessage.description: Your checklist has been saved. We've sent a PDF copy to:

nextSteps.title: Next steps:
items: Review your checklist with your team
Identify gaps and action items
Book a 15-min call to discuss your specific needs
